items.title: History Suggests This Defense Stock Will Outperform the Defense Market
items.author: Rich Smith
items.description: Leidos, through its Dynetics subsidiary, has won a $670 million contract from the U.S. Army to conduct research and development on hypersonic missiles. This contract comes after a strong quarter for Leidos, with the company reporting better-than-expected sales and earnings, an upwards revision to guidance, and a high book-to-bill ratio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Leidos has won a significant contract for hypersonic missile research, which comes on the heels of a strong financial performance in the previous quarter. The company's valuation also appears attractive compared to the broader defense industry.
The article mentions a separate set of defense contracts awarded to Boeing, but the focus is on Leidos and its Dynetics subsidiary.
